channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Pakistani security forces battle to free about 300 hostages aboard a hijacked train
item.description: QUETTA, Pakistan (AP) — Pakistani security forces exchanged gunfire on Wednesday with hundreds of separatist militants as they sought to free about 300 hostages aboard a train in the country’s rugged southwest, officials said.
item.dc:creator: ABDUL SATTAR and MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Center-right party wins most votes in Greenland's parliamentary election as Trump seeks control
item.description: The center-right Demokraatit Party has won the most votes in Greenland elections in a surprise result for an election held in the shadow of President Donald Trump’s stated goal of taking control of the island “one way or the other.
item.dc:creator: Danica Kirka, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Australia won't retaliate against 'unjustified' US tariffs on steel and aluminum
item.description: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese says U.S. tariffs on Australian steel and aluminum are unjustified, but his government will not retaliate with its own tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Yemen's Houthi rebels say 'any Israeli vessel' in nearby Mideast waterways again a target
item.description: Yemen’s Houthi rebels have warned shippers that “any Israeli vessel” traveling through nearby Mideast waters is now a target as Israel continues to block aid to the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pakistani insurgents attack a train carrying hundreds of people and take hostages
item.description: QUETTA, Pakistan (AP) — Pakistani insurgents attacked a passenger train carrying several hundred people as it passed through a tunnel on Tuesday and claimed to have taken more than 100 hostages, though officials later said at least 104 were rescued.
item.dc:creator: ABDUL SATTAR, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Plastics most prevalent in trash flows from Tijuana into US
item.description: According to Alter Terra statistics, by sheer volume, plastic bottles make up 41 percent of all the trash that flows in from Tijuana.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Polls close in Greenland's capital for parliamentary election as Trump seeks control
item.description: NUUK, Greenland (AP) — The polling station in Greenland’s capital closed Tuesday in a parliamentary election that will determine the leaders who will confront U.S. President Donald Trump’s effort to take control of the strategically placed Arctic country.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel kills 8 Palestinians in Gaza as Hamas says ceasefire talks resume
item.description: Israeli fire has killed eight Palestinians in the Gaza Strip and three more in the occupied West Bank over the past 24 hours, Palestinian officials said Tuesday, even as a fragile ceasefire with Hamas in Gaza has largely held since late January.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US resumes military aid and intelligence sharing as Ukraine says it is open to a 30-day ceasefire
item.description: JEDDAH, Saudi Arabia (AP) — The Trump administration lifted its suspension of military aid and intelligence sharing for Ukraine, and Kyiv signaled that it was open to a 30-day ceasefire in the war with Russia, pending Moscow’s agreement, American and Ukrainian officials said Tuesday following talks
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Sudiksha Konanki disappearance timeline from Dominican Republic
item.description: The search involves joint efforts by authorities in the Dominican Republic, the United States and India.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Portugal’s government loses confidence vote, triggering early election amid prolonged instability
item.description: LISBON, Portugal (AP) — Portugal’s minority government lost a confidence vote in parliament Tuesday, forcing its resignation and bringing the European Union country’s third general election in three years.
item.dc:creator: BARRY HATTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A top Romanian court upholds the ban on far-right Georgescu’s presidential bid
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — A top Romanian court on Tuesday rejected an appeal by far-right politician Calin Georgescu to lift a ban on his candidacy for the rerun in May of Romania's presidential election, after the same court annulled last year's election in which Georgescu won the first round.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH and VADIM GHIRDA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK police arrest captain of cargo ship on suspicion of manslaughter over North Sea collision
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British police on Tuesday arrested the captain of a cargo ship on suspicion of manslaughter as they searched for answers about why it hit a tanker transporting jet fuel for the U.S. military off eastern England, setting both vessels ablaze.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ontario agrees to suspend electricity surcharge ahead of talks with US
item.description: The government of Ontario on Tuesday backed off its threat to impose a surcharge on electricity to three U.S. states amid broader trade talks.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: US lifts military freeze on Ukraine in push for 30-day ceasefire
item.description: The U.S. will immediately restore military aid and intelligence sharing to Ukraine following bilateral talks in Jeddah, Saudi Arabia, on Tuesday, with Ukraine expressing "readiness" to accept a 30-day ceasefire proposal. "Ukraine expressed readiness to accept the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9

item.title: FBI joins Dominican Republic search for missing University of Pittsburgh student, police say
item.description: The FBI has joined the Dominican Republic’s police in searching for Sudiksha Konanki, the University of Pittsburgh student who went missing a week ago while on spring break. Konanki, 20, vanished Thursday in Punta Cana, a town located on the easternmost tip of the Dominican Republic.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Judges finds a huge reduction in staff and funding would be legal at African development agency
item.description: A federal judge on Tuesday found that removing most grants and most staff from a U.S. federal agency that invests in African small businesses would be legal.
item.dc:creator: Thalia Beaty, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Greenland voting underway with Trump, independence in headlines
item.description: Greenland's national election will determine control of its parliament, with the ruling Inatsisartut party supporting independence and Trump suggesting the U.S. should take control of the island.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Philippine ex-leader Duterte is being flown to The Hague to face charges of crimes against humanity
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine police arrested former President Rodrigo Duterte in Manila on Tuesday and sent him by plane to the Netherlands to face charges of crimes against humanity at the International Criminal Court, President Ferdinand Marcos said.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bill O'Reilly: Trump should be 'looking to reassure Americans' on economy
item.description: Former Fox News host Bill O'Reilly believes President Trump should be concerned about getting his economic message across as Americans face the rocky impact of his tariff policies and continued inflation, and that he should throw someone out there who's a calm and credible economist to soothe fears.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: British tourist detained in ICE center after visa suspension: Family
item.description: In a statement to NewsNation, an ICE spokesperson said Burke was detained by the agency "for violation of the terms and conditions of her admission.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, NewsNation

item.title: Trump overstepped his constitutional authority in freezing Congress' funding for USAID, judge says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump overstepped his constitutional authority in freezing almost all spending on U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine launches biggest drone attack on Moscow, killing 2, as US talks begin
item.description: Ukraine launched its largest-ever drone attack on Moscow as a delegation met with U.S. officials in Saudi Arabia for talks about ending the war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Syria's government signs a breakthrough deal with Kurdish-led authorities in the northeast
item.description: JABLEH, Syria (AP) — Syria's interim government signed a deal Monday with the Kurdish-led authority that controls the country’s northeast, including a ceasefire and the merging of the main U.S.-backed force there into the Syrian army.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, KAREEM CHEHAYEB and OMAR ALBAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte arrested at airport on ICC warrant for crime against humanity
item.description: Former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte was arrested at Manila Ninoy Aquino International Airport following an order from the International Criminal Court (ICC).
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to expect after South Korea’s Constitutional Court rules on the impeachment of President Yoon
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s Constitutional Court could soon rule on whether to dismiss or reinstate impeached conservative President Yoon Suk Yeol. That doesn't mean the political crisis caused by Yoon’s short-lived imposition of martial law is over.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine will propose a limited ceasefire during talks with the US in Saudi Arabia, officials say
item.description: JEDDAH, Saudi Arabia (AP) — A Ukrainian delegation set to meet with America’s top diplomat in Saudi Arabia about ending the 3-year war with Russia will propose a ceasefire covering the Black Sea and long-range missile strikes, as well as the release of prisoners, two senior Ukrainian officials said
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: For Trump the peace negotiator, might makes right. History offers different lessons
item.description: As President Donald Trump seeks to end wars in the Middle East and Ukraine, his approach to both seems to boil down to giving the stronger party what it wants and pushing the weaker to accept it.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada's conservatives trying to associate Carney with Trudeau
item.description: Conservatives are on the defense after the Liberal Party picked Carney as Canada's next leader, a journalist says.
item.dc:creator: Connell McShane, NewsNation

item.title: Doctors declare Pope Francis no longer in imminent danger due to pneumonia, but remains hospitalized
item.description: ROME (AP) — Doctors said Monday Pope Francis is no longer in imminent danger of death as a result of pneumonia that has kept him hospitalized for nearly a month, but have decided to keep him hospitalized for several more days to receive treatment.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ontario premier says he needs 'to sit down as quickly as possible' with Trump on tariffs
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford said Monday that he needs "to sit down as quickly as possible" with President Trump regarding tariffs. ‘We're seeing inflation happening already, and it's unnecessary. We don't need to do this. We can have the two most prosperous countries in the world.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Pope Francis now 'out of danger from death' as health condition continues to improve
item.description: Vatican officials say that Pope Francis's medical condition has improved and that he is currently “out of danger from death," as of Monday evening.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Doctors declare Pope no longer in imminent danger, but remains hospitalized
item.description: Doctors said Monday that Pope Francis is no longer in imminent danger of death as a result of pneumonia but have decided to keep him hospitalized for several more days to receive treatment.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hamas disregards Trump’s ‘last warning’ over hostage release
item.description: Hamas' response is an apparent dismissal of President Donald Trump's "last warning" issued on social media.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation
